plant_message: I never write TODO comments in code. If something needs doing later, it goes in a GitHub issue with a link in the comment: # See issue #123
recall_queries: How should I mark unfinished work in code?
How do you handle tech debt markers?

plant_message: I prefer explicit over implicit in Python. No star imports, no shadowing builtins, always type-hint function signatures in new code
recall_queries: What are your Python style preferences?

plant_message: For code reviews I want: one concern per comment, always include a code suggestion, never use the word 'just' (it's condescending)
recall_queries: How should code reviews be written?
What to avoid in review comments?

plant_message: Commit messages must start with a type prefix: feat/fix/refactor/test/docs. Body is mandatory for anything non-trivial. No WIP commits to main
recall_queries: What is our commit message format?

plant_message: I want tests to be DAMP not DRY. Duplicate setup code in tests is fine. Shared test fixtures should only be used when the setup is non-trivial (>10 lines)
Inline comments only for non-obvious logic. Function docstrings are optional for private functions, mandatory for public APIs. README updates required for every new feature
I use neovim with LazyVim, tmux for session management, and fish shell. Do not suggest VS Code workflows or bash-specific syntax
Pull requests should be under 400 lines changed. If a feature needs more, split into a stack. I will not review PRs over 800 lines as a rule
I only have availability for meetings Tuesdays and Thursdays between 14:00 and 17:00 UTC. All other time is deep work. Async first
recall_queries: When are you available for meetings?

plant_message: Never swallow exceptions silently. Either re-raise, log at ERROR level with full context, or convert to a domain error. Empty except blocks are a blocking review comment
Boolean variables and functions must start with is_, has_, can_, should_. Event handler functions start with on_. Config values are ALL_CAPS constants
New Python dependencies need a written justification comment in requirements.txt. No dependencies with fewer than 1000 GitHub stars unless security-critical
recall_queries: When can I add a new library?

plant_message: Branch naming: feature/JIRA-123-short-description, bugfix/JIRA-456-description, hotfix/JIRA-789-description. Always branch off main, never off other feature branches
recall_queries: How should feature branches be named?

plant_message: Log lines must be machine-parseable: JSON format with fields timestamp, level, service, trace_id, message. Never log PII. Never log secrets. Use structured logging only
I dislike ORMs for complex queries. Use raw SQL with parameterized queries for anything with JOINs. SQLAlchemy core is fine, SQLAlchemy ORM is discouraged
REST API response envelope: always {data: ..., error: null} or {data: null, error: {code, message}}. Never return different shapes for success vs error. HTTP status codes must be accurate
All user inputs must be validated at the API boundary before entering business logic. Never pass raw request objects into domain functions. Pydantic models for all inputs
recall_queries: How should user input be validated?

plant_message: I follow hexagonal architecture (ports and adapters). Domain logic has zero infrastructure imports. Dependency injection via constructors only, no service locators
recall_queries: Can domain code import database libraries?

plant_message: Premature optimization is forbidden. Profile first with py-spy or pyinstrument before touching hot paths. All performance claims must have benchmark numbers attached
Sprint length is 2 weeks. Thursday afternoon is retrospective, Friday morning is planning. Sprint goal must be written as a user-facing outcome, not a task list
For async Python I use asyncio only, no threads for I/O. Background tasks go through a task queue (Celery or arq), never asyncio.create_task for long-running work
Database migrations must be reversible. Every migration file needs a corresponding downgrade function. Irreversible changes require a team discussion before merging
Secrets are never hardcoded, never in .env files committed to git, never in comments. All secrets come from Vault or AWS Secrets Manager via the startup config loader
I debug with pdb/ipdb for Python, never print statements. For production issues I always pull traces from Jaeger first before looking at logs
PRs must be reviewed within 24 hours of opening on business days. If a reviewer can not meet this, they must delegate or decline. Stale PRs (>3 days, no review) get escalated to the tech lead
recall_queries: How long can a PR wait for review?
